news.title: ACG Metals, Rockfire Resources, Sunda Energy, CelLBxHealth, Poolbeg Pharmaceutical
news.summary: ACG Metals Ltd (LSE:ACG, OTC:ACGAF) beat its full-year oxide production target in just six months, helped by a 64% jump in its realised gold price to $4,838 an ounce. First copper and zinc concentrate production is now expected in August. Rockfire Resources (LSE:ROCK) has returned more strong zinc and silver grades at its Molaoi project in Greece, with preliminary readings showing zinc values as high as 37.7%. The company is fast-tracking feasibility studies and applying for permits to reopen the underground workings. Sunda Energy (AIM:SNDA) has applied for a permit to explore an offshore area of New Zealand's Taranaki Basin covering around 645 square kilometres. The application has triggered a three-month window for competing bids. CelLBxHealth (AIM:CLBX) says it remains on track for revenues of at least £2.1 million this year, up 50% on 2025, as it searches for a new chief executive. Executive chairman Jan Groen says there will be no impact on trading during the recruitment process. Poolbeg Pharma (AIM:POLB) has raised £3.5 million through a placing to fund two clinical trials and strengthen its partnering discussions. The cash runway now extends into the second quarter of 2028.
